# Course contents — History

> The full map of the course — six chapters and an optional history — plus an onramp for engineers arriving from C# and .NET, comparing the CLR and the BEAM and the functional ideas they share.

Chapter map for LLMs/agents. Hub: [/elixir/course](/elixir/course). Links are clean root-relative URLs; this file lists every lesson in the chapter in course order.

## Lessons

- [Elixir for C# developers — History](/elixir/course/csharp): A bridge from .NET to the BEAM: how the two runtimes differ, the functional ideas C# has already adopted, and how the language-ext library maps Option, Either…
- [The evolution of functional languages & runtimes — F0.1](/elixir/course/fp-evolution): From the lambda calculus to LISP, the ML and Haskell branch, and the immutable turn — the lineage Elixir inherited, each idea paired with its Elixir form.
- [The evolution of Erlang, the BEAM & OTP — F0.2](/elixir/course/beam-evolution): Telecom roots and “let it crash”, the reduction-counting scheduler and per-process heaps, and the OTP supervision tree — the runtime Elixir stands on.
